mission.title: radius: Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom CA
mission.description: Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom CA. This issue affects 2+ users.

steps.title: Identify radius troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your radius deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius -n radius --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: No such container: 05ec7676077a547ee6f6f6ae216128fb9b4ab37673c8baf9233`

steps.title: Review radius configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant radius configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius
kubectl get configmap -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=radius
```
## Bug information

I am happy to recreate the "bug" as a "feature request" given it isn't stated as supported anywhere on the docs. 

As an employee at an enterprise company, we have a proxy running on our machines to support internal traffic.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom
steps.description:
Many enterprise customers leverage intermediate root certificate authorities (CAs) to enhance security and control over outgoing traffic originating from their employees' machines, particularly when using a firewall solution. However, when Radius users install Radius on local Kubernetes clusters in
```yaml
❯ rad init
"Selected No to create an application"

Initializing Radius. This may take a minute or two...

✅ Install Radius v0.26.1
   - Kubernetes cluster: rancher-desktop
   - Kubernetes namespace: radius-system
✅ Create new environment default
   - Kubernetes namespace: default
   - Recipe pack: local-dev
✅ Update local configuration

Initialization complete! Have a RAD time 😎

❯ rad recipe list
NAME      TYPE      TEMPLATE KIND  TEMPLATE VERSION  TEMPLATE

❯ k get recipes.radapp.io -A
No resources found
```

steps.title: Confirm Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius -n radius --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n radius --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: No such container: 05ec7676077a547ee6f6f6ae216128fb9b4ab37673c8baf9233` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: Many enterprise customers leverage intermediate root certificate authorities (CAs) to enhance security and control over outgoing traffic originating from their employees' machines, particularly when using a firewall solution. However, when Radius users install Radius on local Kubernetes clusters in their local machines under the corp network, the Radius executables utili
codeSnippets:
❯ rad init
"Selected No to create an application"

Initializing Radius. This may take a minute or two...

✅ Install Radius v0.26.1
   - Kubernetes cluster: rancher-desktop
   - Kubernetes namespace: radius-system
✅ Create new environment default
   - Kubernetes namespace: default
   - Recipe pack: local-dev
✅ Update local configuration

Initialization complete! Have a RAD time 😎

❯ rad recipe list
NAME      TYPE      TEMPLATE KIND  TEMPLATE VERSION  TEMPLATE

❯ k get recipes.radapp.io -A
No resources found

❯ rad recipe register default --template-path ghcr.io/radius-project/recipes/local-dev/rediscaches:0.26.6 --resource-type Applications.Datastores/redisCaches --template-kind bicep
Successfully linked recipe "default" to environment "default"

apiVersion: v1
data:
  HTTP_PROXY: http://corp-proxy.com
  HTTPS_PROXY: http://corp-proxy.com
  NO_PROXY: .radius-system,controller.radius-system,ucp.radius-system,bicep-de.radius-system,applications-rp.radius-system,localhost,127.0.0.1,10.0.0.0/8,kubernetes
  http_proxy: http://corp-proxy.com
  https_proxy: http://corp-proxy.com
  no_proxy: .radius-system,controller.radius-system,ucp.radius-system,bicep-de.radius-system,applications-rp.radius-system,localhost,127.0.0.1,10.0.0.0/8,kubernetes
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: proxy-env

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with radius installed or the issue environment reproducible.
